metadata.scope: Retained vendor package and backend-relevant QwenVoice integration surfaces

entries.Title: In-tree owned snapshot
entries.Local behavior: The package is copied into QwenVoice and maintained as source, not as a submodule or remote SPM dependency.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream is the standalone source repository and package.
entries.Impact: Deterministic source and local patch ownership; higher sync burden.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; rename conceptually as an owned Qwen3 runtime.

entries.Title: Qwen3-TTS-only product surface
entries.Local behavior: Only MLXAudioCore, MLXAudioCodecs, and MLXAudioTTS products remain.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream exposes Core, Codecs, TTS, STT, VAD, LID, STS, UI, G2P, an aggregate product, and command-line tools.
entries.Impact: Smaller compile surface and less unrelated churn; not a generally reusable audio SDK.

entries.Title: Removed multi-model targets
entries.Local behavior: STT, STS, VAD, LID, G2P, UI, and Tools source targets were deleted from the snapshot.
entries.Upstream behavior: All remain active upstream.
entries.Impact: Approximately 36K of the imported ~49K LOC were removed according to repository metadata.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; summarize deletions by target rather than pretending to track every deleted line.
entries.Confidence: Confirmed by source and specialization commit

entries.Title: Mimi primitives only
entries.Local behavior: The codec target retains the Mimi Transformer/Conv/Quantization/Seanet primitives used by Qwen3-TTS.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream contains many codec families, including BigVGAN, DACVAE, Descript DAC, Encodec, Fish codecs, SNAC, Vocos, and others.
entries.Impact: Reduces package scope while preserving the required Qwen speech-tokenizer substrate.

entries.Title: AudioCodecModel and Mimi wrapper omitted
entries.Local behavior: The generic AudioCodecModel protocol and unused Mimi wrapper were removed because Qwen3 directly uses lower-level primitives.
entries.Upstream behavior: The imported baseline carried those generic surfaces.
entries.Impact: Less unused abstraction; more divergence from upstream package layout.

entries.Title: Unused Core utilities removed
entries.Local behavior: AudioPlayer, AudioSessionManager, PCMStreamConverter, and UnigramTokenizer were removed from the vendor package.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream retains general-purpose Core utilities.
entries.Impact: Smaller accidental API surface.

entries.Title: Exact MLX pins
entries.Local behavior: The vendor package pins mlx-swift 0.30.6 and mlx-swift-lm 2.30.6 exactly.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream uses compatible version ranges and a newer MLX-LM major line.
entries.Impact: Reproducible shipping substrate, but increasing migration distance.
entries.Disposition: Keep exact production pins; add a scheduled compatibility lane.

entries.Title: swift-huggingface 0.9.0 exact pin
entries.Local behavior: The fork pins swift-huggingface 0.9.0.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream declares a compatible range beginning at 0.8.1.
entries.Impact: Local dependency is newer in this dimension, but exact-pinned.
entries.Disposition: Keep until a compatibility branch proves an upgrade.

entries.Title: Removed swift-transformers and MLXLLM from vendor target
entries.Local behavior: The Qwen-only package no longer declares swift-transformers or the MLXLLM product.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream TTS and codec targets use Transformers and MLXLLM for the broad model set.
entries.Impact: Fewer dependencies and less compile/link surface.

entries.Title: Swift tools 6.1 versus upstream 6.2
entries.Local behavior: The local vendor manifest declares Swift tools 6.1.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream declares Swift tools 6.2.
entries.Impact: Potential future manifest/API drift.
entries.Disposition: Review with the MLX dependency upgrade, not independently.

entries.Title: No vendor SwiftPM test target
entries.Local behavior: The specialized Package.swift defines production targets only.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has direct package tests, including Qwen3-TTS fixture tests.
entries.Impact: The fork has more Qwen-specific behavior but less direct deterministic coverage.

entries.Title: No repository-owned Python inference backend
entries.Local behavior: The product backend is native Swift/MLX; Python bridge/runtime paths are intentionally prohibited.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream Swift is also native, but the QwenVoice rule is a product-level packaging invariant.
entries.Impact: Supports local native distribution and avoids bundling Python.

entries.Title: Standalone upstream tools removed; product CLI lives outside vendor
entries.Local behavior: The vendor executable targets are removed, while VocelloCLI links the product engine through QwenVoiceCore.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream ships generic TTS/STT/codec/STS/LID executables.
entries.Impact: One product-aware CLI rather than generic package tools.

entries.Title: prepareForGeneration protocol requirement
entries.Local behavior: SpeechGenerationModel has an async prepareForGeneration operation with a default one-token warmup.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream SpeechGenerationModel does not expose this preparation requirement.
entries.Impact: Separates prewarming from user-visible synthesis.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; test cancellation and cache semantics.

entries.Title: Structured AudioGenerationFinishReason
entries.Local behavior: Completion distinguishes eos, maxTokens, cancelled, and failed.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream generic API returns audio/events without this mode-specific completion object.
entries.Impact: Prevents token-cap truncation from being silently treated as success.

entries.Title: AudioGenerationCompletion result
entries.Local behavior: Full-result mode returns audio, optional generation info, and finish reason.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream full-result generation returns MLXArray.
entries.Impact: Makes terminal state part of the type contract.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; upstreamable as an additive API.

entries.Title: Explicit Custom Voice APIs
entries.Local behavior: The optimized protocol has prepare, stream, and full-result Custom Voice operations with separate speaker and instruction arguments.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream routes Custom Voice through the generic voice string, including a 'speaker, instruction' compatibility convention.
entries.Impact: Removes ambiguous string parsing and enables mode-specific policy.

entries.Title: Explicit Voice Design APIs
entries.Local behavior: Voice Design has separate preparation, stream, and full-result methods.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses the generic SpeechGenerationModel surface.
entries.Impact: Clear product contract and independent quality policy.

entries.Title: Explicit Voice Clone APIs
entries.Local behavior: Clone prompt creation, preparation, stream, and full-result generation are first-class operations.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has reusable reference conditioning but not the same persistent product artifact and mode-specific API.
entries.Impact: Enables Saved Voices and repeatable clone conditioning.

entries.Title: Custom Voice prewarm depth control
entries.Local behavior: A separate protocol allows the caller to select custom prewarm depth.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent upstream product policy.
entries.Impact: Avoids expensive decoder-bucket work when it will not amortize.

entries.Title: Chunk timing event
entries.Local behavior: AudioGeneration includes a chunkTimings event immediately before the corresponding audio chunk.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has token, info, and audio events only.
entries.Impact: Enables per-chunk attribution and regression analysis.

entries.Title: PCM stream termination cancels producer
entries.Local behavior: The local PCM buffer stream stores its producer task, checks cancellation around conversion, and cancels it on stream termination.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream launches an unretained task and has no matching onTermination cancellation in this helper.
entries.Impact: Prevents abandoned helper streams from continuing work.

entries.Title: SpeechGenerationModelDiagnosticsProvider integration
entries.Local behavior: Qwen3TTS exposes load timings, preparation timings, boolean flags, string flags, and reset hooks consumed by Core.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent generic upstream diagnostics contract.
entries.Impact: Provides forensic visibility at the cost of stringly typed contracts.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; replace raw string dictionaries with typed schema.

entries.Title: KVCacheDiagnostics type
entries.Local behavior: Chunk telemetry carries cache type, sequence length, layers, attention heads, KV heads, head dimension, estimated dtype bytes, and estimated footprint.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream event types do not expose KV cache diagnostics.
entries.Impact: Supports trend detection; estimate is explicitly not allocator ground truth.

entries.Title: MimiDecoderStepTimings type
entries.Local behavior: Per-frame decoder timings cover quantizer, pre-conv, transformer, upsample, initial conv, decoder blocks, output Snake, output conv, and total.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream does not expose these substages.
entries.Impact: Localizes decoder spikes.

entries.Title: ChunkSubstageTimings type
entries.Local behavior: Each chunk can carry talker, code predictor, decoder, eval, EOS-read, audio materialization, KV, and Mimi breakdowns.
entries.Upstream behavior: No upstream counterpart.
entries.Impact: Rich performance evidence; adds API surface to a shared enum.

entries.Title: Backward-compatible KV diagnostic decoding
entries.Local behavior: KVCacheDiagnostics decodes older rows without kvHeadCount by falling back to headCount.
entries.Upstream behavior: No upstream equivalent because the type is local.
entries.Impact: Preserves historical telemetry readability.

entries.Title: Revision-aware Hugging Face resolution
entries.Local behavior: ModelUtils accepts an explicit revision and passes it to downloadSnapshot.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream generic ModelUtils hardcodes main in its common download path.
entries.Impact: Allows deterministic checkpoint resolution.

entries.Title: Revision-separated model cache directory
entries.Local behavior: Non-main revisions are encoded into a cache-safe model-directory suffix.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses a repository-derived directory without revision separation.
entries.Impact: Prevents different revisions from aliasing one cache directory.

entries.Title: QwenPreparedLoadBehavior
entries.Local behavior: Loading can trust a prepared checkpoint, skip repeat directory preparation, independently include encoders, and skip speech-tokenizer parameter evaluation.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream exposes ordinary repository/local-directory loading without this capability profile.
entries.Impact: Makes constrained-device and already-validated loads configurable.

entries.Title: preparePreparedDirectory API
entries.Local behavior: Qwen tokenizer preparation can be run before model construction.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream generates/loads tokenizer during normal model-directory loading.
entries.Impact: Supports staged model delivery and validation.

entries.Title: Qwen3-TTS-only TTS router
entries.Local behavior: TTS rejects all non-Qwen3-TTS model types.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream routes many TTS families.
entries.Impact: Eliminates unused model registry paths.

entries.Title: Tokenizer load overlaps talker loading
entries.Local behavior: loadModelContents starts tokenizer loading with async let while it loads talker components.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream performs a simpler sequential model-directory load.
entries.Impact: Reduces cold-load wall time where work overlaps safely.

entries.Title: Direct strict tokenizer load with fallback
entries.Local behavior: The loader constructs PreTrainedTokenizer directly from tokenizer files with strict mode, falling back to AutoTokenizer.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream primarily uses AutoTokenizer in the reviewed Qwen loader.
entries.Impact: Avoids repeated generalized auto-resolution and records which path was used.

entries.Title: Prepared tokenizer component cache
entries.Local behavior: Tokenizers are cached by canonical prepared-directory key, with smaller iOS limits.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has a reference-audio cache but no equivalent prepared-component cache.
entries.Impact: Avoids repeated tokenizer reconstruction.

entries.Title: Prepared speech-tokenizer cache
entries.Local behavior: Speech tokenizers are cached separately by prepared directory and encoder inclusion.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent upstream cache.
entries.Impact: Allows decoder-only and full-capability variants without key collision.

entries.Title: Decoder-only speech-tokenizer loading
entries.Local behavior: When the encoder is omitted, encoder weights are filtered before sanitization/update.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream loads the speech tokenizer as a full model-directory component.
entries.Impact: Reduces load cost for modes that do not need reference encoding.

entries.Title: Qwen3 12 Hz speech-tokenizer config contract validation
entries.Local behavior: The local loader rejects a tokenizer configuration that does not match expected Qwen3-TTS 12 Hz structure.
entries.Upstream behavior: The reviewed upstream loader decodes configuration and loads weights without this same product contract check.
entries.Impact: Fails early on incompatible artifacts.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; upstreamable as model-specific validation.

entries.Title: Prefer direct model.safetensors then merge shards
entries.Local behavior: loadWeights first checks a named direct safetensors file, otherwise merges all safetensors in the directory.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream enumerates and merges model-directory safetensors.
entries.Impact: Avoids unnecessary directory merge work for canonical prepared layouts.

entries.Title: Explicit dictionary/pair release between load phases
entries.Local behavior: Temporary weight dictionaries and parameter-pair arrays are cleared around updates and cache clears.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses a simpler load path with fewer explicit release points.
entries.Impact: Narrows transient load high-water where references are genuinely distinct.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; retain only evidence-backed release points.

entries.Title: Layer-batched talker evaluation
entries.Local behavior: Talker and code-predictor parameters are materialized in bounded layer batches.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream evaluates whole component parameter collections more directly.
entries.Impact: Controls load-time materialization pressure.

entries.Title: Batched speech-tokenizer parameter evaluation
entries.Local behavior: Speech-tokenizer parameter arrays are evaluated in batches with cache clears between batches.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent bounded evaluator in the reviewed upstream loader.
entries.Impact: Reduces peak materialization pressure.

entries.Title: Relaxed unused-key verification only for trusted prepared checkpoint
entries.Local behavior: The trusted prepared path uses noUnusedKeys verification while the ordinary path uses all.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses ordinary full verification for its standard load path.
entries.Impact: Avoids duplicate expensive validation only after Core has established artifact trust.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; include runtime/dependency ABI in trust signature.

entries.Title: Prepared-load diagnostic event sink
entries.Local behavior: The loader emits named before/after/failure events and structured string details.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent upstream sink.
entries.Impact: Makes cold-load failures diagnosable.

entries.Title: Requested component may be returned without loaded weights
entries.Local behavior: If speaker-encoder or speech-tokenizer weight maps are empty, the code can log a warning and still retain/return an initialized component object.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream also has permissive warning paths, but the local capability-profile machinery can make presence look authoritative.
entries.Impact: A corrupted or incomplete prepared directory could expose randomly initialized components.
entries.Disposition: Fix: throw or return nil when a requested learned component has no verified weights.

entries.Title: Prepared checkpoint trust marker and fingerprinting
entries.Local behavior: QwenVoiceCore records descriptor/config/runtime profile and file metadata/digests to avoid repeating full preparation checks.
entries.Upstream behavior: This product-level trust layer is outside upstream.
entries.Impact: Makes large local checkpoint reuse practical while retaining invalidation evidence.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; bind to exact MLX/LM versions and fork schema.

entries.Title: Scalar-or-array speaker ID compatibility
entries.Local behavior: The local decoder first attempts [String:[Int]], then falls back from [String:Int] to one-element arrays.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream uses a typed SpkIdValue enum for the same checkpoint variability.
entries.Impact: Supports both Base and Custom Voice config shapes.

entries.Title: String-or-bool dialect compatibility
entries.Local behavior: The local decoder accepts String or Bool and retains only string dialect names.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream uses a typed SpkDialectValue preserving both forms.
entries.Impact: Handles mixed checkpoint JSON; local representation loses an explicit false value.
entries.Disposition: Port upstream's typed representation when convenient.

entries.Title: Unsupported speaker is a typed generation error
entries.Local behavior: Explicit Custom Voice preparation requires a nonempty speaker ID and throws when it is absent from the map.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream generic preparation can warn and continue without a speaker embedding in some compatibility paths.
entries.Impact: Avoids silently generating with the wrong conditioning.

entries.Title: Dialect can override Auto/Chinese language ID
entries.Local behavior: For configured dialect speakers, the local resolver maps the dialect to the codec language ID.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream also supports dialect metadata, but through its generic prompt path.
entries.Impact: Preserves expected dialect token conditioning.

entries.Title: 0.6B Custom Voice instruction suppression
entries.Local behavior: The local runtime disables Custom Voice instruction control for the 0.6B Custom Voice configuration.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream generic parsing accepts speaker/instruction without the same product guard.
entries.Impact: Prevents unsupported conditioning from being presented as effective.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; document by model capability.

entries.Title: Conditioning-prefix LRU cache
entries.Local behavior: Custom and Design control prefixes are cached independently using normalized model/mode/language/speaker/instruction keys.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has a reference-audio identity cache, not this product prefix cache.
entries.Impact: Avoids repeated tokenization and embedding construction.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; replace string concatenation with typed keys.

entries.Title: Streaming trailing-text versus full-text nonstreaming modes
entries.Local behavior: The generation core has an explicit Qwen3TextConditioningMode and full-result APIs select fullTextNonStreaming.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has one generic generation assembly path.
entries.Impact: Separates preview latency behavior from canonical final waveform behavior.

entries.Title: Versioned Qwen3TTSVoiceClonePrompt
entries.Local behavior: Clone conditioning stores optional reference codes, speaker embedding, transcript, ICL/x-vector mode, and metadata.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has reusable in-memory reference conditioning but not the same product persistence format.
entries.Impact: Enables Saved Voices and cross-run reuse.

entries.Title: Artifact metadata matching
entries.Local behavior: Metadata can bind model ID, language, source fingerprint, transcript hash/presence, clone mode, runtime profile, and creation time.
entries.Upstream behavior: No upstream persistent metadata contract.
entries.Impact: Invalidates stale prompt artifacts when selected model/reference changes.

entries.Title: Production directory-level atomic publication
entries.Local behavior: QwenVoiceCore writes a complete prompt into a unique sibling temporary directory, then moves or atomically replaces the destination.
entries.Upstream behavior: Not applicable upstream.
entries.Impact: Prevents a partially written manifest/tensor set from replacing the prior complete artifact.
entries.Notes: Correction to the earlier report: production publication is atomic. The low-level serializer alone is not.

entries.Title: Low-level serializer is not independently transactional
entries.Local behavior: Qwen3TTSVoiceClonePrompt.write writes manifest and tensor files in sequence; safety depends on the Core staging wrapper.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent persistent serializer upstream.
entries.Impact: A future direct caller could bypass atomic publication.
entries.Disposition: Make serializer internal or rename/document it as staging-only.

entries.Title: Tensor structural/content validation missing
entries.Local behavior: Loading checks schema and metadata but does not verify expected tensor key count, dtype, dimensions, file length, or content digest.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent persistent artifact upstream.
entries.Impact: Corrupted or mismatched tensors can fail later or condition the wrong voice.
entries.Disposition: Fix: manifest file hashes, sizes, keys, dtypes, shapes, and mode requirements.

entries.Title: Normalized-reference, decoded-audio, conditioning, and prompt LRUs
entries.Local behavior: Core caches multiple clone pipeline stages and fingerprints source audio with SHA-256.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream's model cache is narrower and based on MLXArray object identity for reference context.
entries.Impact: Avoids repeated normalization, decoding, encoding, and prompt construction.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; add memory accounting and typed cache keys.

entries.Title: ICL and x-vector-only prompt creation
entries.Local behavior: The clone prompt builder validates transcript requirements and can omit reference codes for x-vector-only mode.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream reference-conditioning APIs focus on the generic raw reference path.
entries.Impact: Supports distinct clone quality/memory modes.

entries.Title: Qwen3TTSGenerationGate
entries.Local behavior: Preparation and generation operations share a FIFO actor gate to protect mutable decoder/cache state.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream relies on caller discipline and model-local mutable state without this same explicit product gate.
entries.Impact: Prevents concurrent mutation of model and streaming decoder state.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; fix transfer cancellation race.

entries.Title: Generation-gate transfer race
entries.Local behavior: After a queued continuation is resumed, a final cancellation check can throw after ownership transferred but before release protection begins, leaving the gate held.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent gate upstream.
entries.Impact: Can indefinitely block later preparation and generation calls.
entries.Disposition: High priority fix with deterministic cancellation injection test.

entries.Title: Stream producer cancellation propagation
entries.Local behavior: Mode-specific streams retain producer tasks and cancel them from continuation termination.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream Qwen stream construction is simpler and historically had weaker termination linkage.
entries.Impact: Stops generation when the consumer abandons the stream.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; add cancellation tests at every suspension/sync boundary.

entries.Title: Decoder streaming state reset in defer
entries.Local behavior: Streaming generation resets decoder state before use and in defer after completion/error.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream also resets around streaming; local has more mode-specific entry points.
entries.Impact: Prevents state leakage between generations.

entries.Title: Diagnostics guarded by NSLock
entries.Local behavior: Load/preparation diagnostic dictionaries are lock-protected.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has little corresponding mutable diagnostic state.
entries.Impact: Avoids unsynchronized reads across adapter/executor boundaries.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; typed immutable snapshots would be cleaner.

entries.Title: Multiple @unchecked Sendable wrappers
entries.Local behavior: MLX arrays, cached prefixes, clone prompts, and model adapters cross concurrency boundaries under explicit unchecked contracts.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream also uses unchecked sendability in model code, but the local surface is larger.
entries.Impact: Correctness depends on single-owner/gated access invariants not enforced by the type system.
entries.Disposition: Keep only where necessary; add debug assertions and forced-concurrency tests.

entries.Title: Qwen3TTSMemoryCaches.clearAll
entries.Local behavior: One public async operation clears prepared components, conditioning prefixes, decoder-bucket records, and the MLX cache.
entries.Upstream behavior: No equivalent upstream aggregate cache control.
entries.Impact: Allows host memory-pressure policy to purge fork-level caches.

entries.Title: Decoder bucket precompilation
entries.Local behavior: Prewarm exercises 4/1/2/3-token decoder shapes and records a warmed-key cache.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream does not precompile these product decoder buckets.
entries.Impact: Shifts first-use shape compilation ahead of playback.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; gate by prompt/device policy.

entries.Title: Mode/device-dependent prewarm depth
entries.Local behavior: Core can skip bucket or stream-step warming for short prompts or constrained tiers.
entries.Upstream behavior: No upstream product policy.
entries.Impact: Avoids paying cold-work that will not amortize.

entries.Title: Minimum two generated code tokens before EOS
entries.Local behavior: EOS is withheld until the production minimum generated-code count is reached.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream permits EOS whenever the sampled token is available.
entries.Impact: Avoids degenerate empty/near-empty outputs.

entries.Title: Token-cap is recorded and surfaced
entries.Local behavior: The loop records eos/token_cap/failed and full-result APIs map it into AudioGenerationFinishReason.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream stops at effectiveMaxTokens and returns generated audio without the same structured completion.
entries.Impact: Allows Core to discard truncated output.

entries.Title: Streaming retains pending chunk codes only
entries.Local behavior: Streaming appends code tensors to pendingStreamCodes and clears after emission; full-result mode separately retains all codes.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream keeps generatedCodes for the entire generation while also decoding slices.
entries.Impact: Makes streaming peak much flatter with output length.

entries.Title: Reserve capacities for code/token buffers
entries.Local behavior: The local loop reserves capacity based on effective token budgets and chunk sizes.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses ordinary dynamically growing arrays.
entries.Impact: Reduces Swift allocation churn.

entries.Title: Incremental repetition token Set
entries.Local behavior: Unique generated token IDs are maintained incrementally and mirrored into sampler scratch.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream reconstructs Array(Set(generatedTokens)) inside sampling.
entries.Impact: Avoids per-token hashing and allocations.

entries.Title: Qwen3SamplerScratch reuse
entries.Local behavior: Arange, zero, negative-infinity, EOS index, suppression pairs, and repetition IDs are reused.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream allocates these arrays within sampleToken.
entries.Impact: Reduces graph-build and allocation overhead in talker and 15 code-predictor draws per frame.

entries.Title: Temperature applied before top-p/min-p filtering
entries.Local behavior: Logits are temperature-scaled immediately after greedy handling; categorical samples the already-scaled logits.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream filters untempered logits and divides only at final categorical sampling.
entries.Impact: Probability truncation reflects the actual tempered distribution.
entries.Disposition: Strong upstream candidate with deterministic tests.

entries.Title: EOS logit preserved through truncation filters
entries.Local behavior: The original EOS logit is restored after top-k/top-p/min-p filtering when EOS is allowed.
entries.Upstream behavior: Reviewed upstream also preserves EOS in its current Qwen sampler.
entries.Impact: Prevents truncation filters from permanently suppressing normal completion.

entries.Title: Independent talker/subtalker overrides
entries.Local behavior: Environment/test policy can override subtalker temperature/top-k/top-p independently from the talker.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream feeds the same generic generation settings through both paths.
entries.Impact: Enables controlled delivery A/B experiments.
entries.Disposition: Keep dev-only; pass typed policy instead of reading environment in vendor code.

entries.Title: Expressive/Balanced/Consistent request variation
entries.Local behavior: Core maps request variation to talker temperature/top-p, with environment values taking precedence for development.
entries.Upstream behavior: No upstream product variation concept.
entries.Impact: Provides user-facing reproducibility/variation control.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; avoid process-global mutable variation if concurrent requests are ever allowed.

entries.Title: Per-request MLX random seed
entries.Local behavior: QwenVoiceCore applies the request seed before generation.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream generic calls do not expose the same product seed contract.
entries.Impact: Supports reproducible takes and benchmark comparisons.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; add fixed-seed trace tests.

entries.Title: Local default maxTokens is 2048
entries.Local behavior: Qwen3TTSModel.defaultGenerationParameters uses 2048, aligned with QwenVoiceBackendCore officialQualityDefault.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream Qwen3TTS default is 4096.
entries.Impact: Bounds product latency/memory and defines when rotating KV can become active.
entries.Disposition: Keep local while product cap remains intentional; document mismatch.

entries.Title: Mode/profile-specific token budget policy
entries.Local behavior: Effective max tokens and minimum codes can be resolved by streaming mode, Custom Voice profile, and speed profile.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses min(maxTokens, max(75, targetTokens*6)).
entries.Impact: Allows evidence-driven latency policy without changing generic parameters.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; make policies typed and tested.

entries.Title: Larger post-first chunk sizes
entries.Local behavior: After the first low-latency chunk, Design/Clone and profile-controlled Custom Voice use larger decode chunks.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses one fixed chunk size derived from streamingInterval.
entries.Impact: Balances first-audio latency against decoder efficiency.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; verify waveform partition invariance.

entries.Title: Configurable MLX cache-clear cadence
entries.Local behavior: Cache clearing cadence is resolved by speed profile/explicit override and can be zero for full-result quality paths.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream clears Memory cache every 50 generation steps.
entries.Impact: Avoids unnecessary cache churn while retaining constrained-tier controls.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; benchmark by device tier.

entries.Title: full/eos-only/deferred stream-step eval policy
entries.Local behavior: The local loop can explicitly evaluate both inputEmbeds/EOS, EOS only, or defer; full remains production default after measurement.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream always evaluates inputEmbeds and EOS each step.
entries.Impact: Supports attribution experiments; deferred did not eliminate compute.
entries.Disposition: Keep as diagnostic policy; do not expose casually.

entries.Title: Optional quantized talker KV cache
entries.Local behavior: Talker makeCache can return QuantizedKVCache and attention dispatches through attentionWithCacheUpdate.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses KVCacheSimple and direct SDPA.
entries.Impact: Experimental memory lever; can alter numerics and needs quality gates.

entries.Title: Optional rotating talker KV cache
entries.Local behavior: A cache pins the conditioning prefix and rotates generated audio-code tokens beyond an environment-selected window.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream exposes only simple talker cache in this model.
entries.Impact: Dormant insurance capability; current 2048 token cap makes a 2048 window inert.
entries.Disposition: Keep env-only; do not claim active RAM savings.

entries.Title: Cache implementation type tracking
entries.Local behavior: Talker records whether the last cache was simple, quantized, or rotating.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream does not expose this telemetry.
entries.Impact: Makes cache-policy evidence interpretable.

entries.Title: Fused MLXFast.RoPE
entries.Local behavior: CodePredictorAttention applies MLXFast.RoPE with offset instead of constructing cos/sin tables and manual rotate-half operations.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream computes rotary embeddings and applies manual q/k rotation.
entries.Impact: Reduces graph operations and kernel launches in the repeated code-predictor loop.
entries.Provenance: Recorded in optimization ledger as P3 / `f3cd2aa`.

entries.Title: Per-generation causal-mask memoization
entries.Local behavior: CodePredictorStepConstants caches the pass-0 mask because the code-predictor cache is reset every frame.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream rebuilds the causal mask when seqLen > 1.
entries.Impact: Avoids repeated graph-build work.

entries.Title: Compiled shapeless SwiGLU
entries.Local behavior: Local Code Predictor and Talker use compile(shapeless:) for the elementwise SwiGLU interior.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream already does the same.
entries.Impact: Important active optimization, but should not be credited as fork-only.

entries.Title: In-loop audio chunk async evaluation
entries.Local behavior: Non-final streaming chunks can be async-evaluated so decoder materialization overlaps later token work.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream synchronously evaluates each emitted audio chunk.
entries.Impact: Pipelines decoder work with the autoregressive loop.
entries.Disposition: Keep local; preserve final-chunk ordering barrier.

entries.Title: Final trailing chunk forced synchronous
entries.Local behavior: The trailing decoder chunk is evaluated synchronously before generation returns.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream synchronously evaluates all chunks, so it does not have the local async handoff race.
entries.Impact: Closes a race where final-file handoff could precede playback queue drain.

entries.Title: Dedicated full-result generation for Custom/Design/Clone
entries.Local behavior: Mode-specific full-result methods select full-text conditioning and return structured completion.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream's generic generate path does not make this product distinction explicit.
entries.Impact: Treats streaming preview and canonical persisted waveform as separate policies.

entries.Title: Input-side overlap-and-discard transposed convolution
entries.Local behavior: DecoderBlockUpsample keeps one input frame as context, recomputes a composed output, and discards the repeated prefix.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream accumulates an output overflow tail and adds it into the next chunk.
entries.Impact: Removes chunk-boundary floating-point accumulation drift and makes output partition-invariant.
entries.Disposition: Strong upstream candidate with partition tests.

entries.Title: Exact block-count fast-path guard
entries.Local behavior: The optimized decoder block step requires exactly five layers; any other shape uses a count-agnostic fallback.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses the ordinary block implementation without this local explicit guard/commented invariant.
entries.Impact: Avoids silently dropping future residual layers.

entries.Title: streamingStepWithTimings
entries.Local behavior: The speech decoder can return waveform plus detailed Mimi decoder timings.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream exposes the ordinary streaming step.
entries.Impact: Supports per-frame and per-chunk decoder attribution.

entries.Title: Encoder can be omitted at construction/load
entries.Local behavior: Qwen3TTSSpeechTokenizer has an includeEncoder capability path used by prepared load profiles.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream model-directory load generally constructs the full configured tokenizer.
entries.Impact: Reduces resident model surface when cloning/reference encoding is unavailable or unnecessary.

entries.Title: Explicit reset across all incremental layers
entries.Local behavior: Initial/output conv buffers, decoder blocks, and upsample input context are reset between streams.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream resets its overflow/buffer state under its own algorithm.
entries.Impact: Required for independent generations.

entries.Title: Local Mimi wrappers remain on older MLX API style
entries.Local behavior: The fork retains the imported Conv wrappers compatible with MLX 0.30.6.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream has refactored Mimi convolution wrappers around newer MLXNN Conv1d/ConvTransposed1d classes.
entries.Impact: An upgrade blocker, not a present correctness defect.
entries.Disposition: Evaluate only with the MLX/LM compatibility branch.

entries.Title: QwenVoice-specific closure adapter
entries.Local behavior: UnsafeSpeechGenerationModel converts the fork protocols into QwenVoiceCore closures and applies product parameter policies.
entries.Upstream behavior: No upstream equivalent.
entries.Impact: Allows a single-owner model to cross actor boundaries, but expands unchecked contracts.

entries.Title: Fork-specific types leak above BackendCore
entries.Local behavior: QwenVoiceCore and several app/service targets directly link MLXAudioCore/TTS and use fork-specific protocols/types.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream is itself the package boundary.
entries.Impact: Increases blast radius of vendor API changes.
entries.Disposition: Constrain fork-specific APIs to BackendCore; neutral MLXArray/Core exceptions may remain explicit.

entries.Title: Product streaming WAV/final-file pipeline outside vendor
entries.Local behavior: QwenVoiceCore writes streamed/final audio with product-owned atomic file and telemetry behavior.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream provides generic AudioUtils and StreamingWAVWriter.
entries.Impact: Separates package generation from durable application artifacts.

entries.Title: Local vendor StreamingWAVWriter lacks upstream buffer reuse
entries.Local behavior: The specialized vendor still allocates a new AVAudioPCMBuffer per generic writer chunk.
entries.Upstream behavior: Current upstream reuses a buffer and accepts MLXArray/data writes.
entries.Impact: Low production impact because the product uses a different writer.

entries.Title: Host-driven cache trimming and platform policy
entries.Local behavior: Core resolves device-tier memory behavior, invokes fork cache clearing, and controls prewarm/streaming policies.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream package does not own product host memory admission or XPC/iOS policy.
entries.Impact: Adapts one model runtime to 8 GB Macs and iPhones.

entries.Title: XPC engine isolation
entries.Local behavior: macOS runs generation in a service for crash isolation and retire/unload control.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream is an in-process SDK.
entries.Impact: Process isolation and lifecycle control; not a vendor-patch difference by itself.

entries.Title: In-process iOS engine
entries.Local behavior: iOS uses the same backend in-process under iOS memory policy.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream is a library and does not dictate process model.
entries.Impact: Avoids extension memory-cap constraints.

entries.Title: Streaming versus nonstreaming retention dominates peak
entries.Local behavior: Repository benchmarks report streaming Speed paths around ~2.4–3.8 GB versus roughly ~7–8 GB for old full-history nonstreaming runs.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream source retains full generated code history in its streaming loop, but no equivalent Vocello hardware matrix was reviewed.
entries.Impact: Supports the source-level pending-chunk design.
entries.Disposition: Treat as hardware-specific evidence, not a universal number.

entries.Title: Streaming-default Speed reaches approximately realtime on reference 8 GB Mac
entries.Local behavior: The committed streaming-default baseline reports Custom/Design Speed RTF around 0.95–1.04 with all QC passing.
entries.Impact: Demonstrates product viability on the recorded machine/configuration.
entries.Disposition: Preserve baseline metadata; do not generalize to all devices.

entries.Title: Swift timers around lazy MLX ops are not GPU-stage timings
entries.Local behavior: The repository explicitly states that stepEval contains fused compute while talker/codePred timers largely measure graph construction.
entries.Impact: Prevents incorrect optimization conclusions.
entries.Disposition: Retain this caveat in all performance reports.

entries.Title: Compiling quantized per-frame graph regressed warm RTF
entries.Local behavior: A validation spike reportedly regressed warm RTF by about 5% and was reverted.
entries.Impact: Explains why the fork compiles only small elementwise regions rather than the whole quantized loop.
entries.Disposition: Do not reintroduce without a new MLX implementation or different trace-constant strategy.

entries.Title: Eager talkerSourceWeights release measured no benefit
entries.Local behavior: A reordered release was tested, found within noise, and reverted.
entries.Impact: Avoids crediting a nonexistent active optimization.
entries.Disposition: Leave reverted; re-test only on speaker-encoder-heavy clone load.

entries.Title: Rotating KV produced negligible RAM benefit under current cap
entries.Local behavior: Measurements found talker KV to be tens of MB and a 2048 window cannot rotate before the 2048 token cap.
entries.Impact: Corrects earlier estimates that attributed gigabytes to the talker KV.
entries.Disposition: Keep off by default; do not market as a current memory optimization.

entries.Title: Punctuation-aware audioQC is Core policy, not model optimization
entries.Local behavior: Core budgets long pauses against punctuation and flags only excess/egregious gaps.
entries.Impact: Reduces false positives without changing generated waveform.
entries.Disposition: Keep separate from model-backend delta claims.

entries.Title: Repository A/B reports negligible engine cost
entries.Local behavior: On-device evidence records no measurable RTF or peak-memory cost within noise for enabled live preview.
entries.Impact: Supports retaining live playback, but applies only to the tested device/configuration.

entries.Title: Upstream tiny Qwen fixtures and direct tests
entries.Local behavior: The fork does not include a dedicated equivalent test target.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream tests reusable reference conditioning, direct/stream generation, raw compatibility, and Custom Voice separation.
entries.Impact: High-value evidence that can be adapted without importing unrelated models.

entries.Title: Typed SpkIdValue and SpkDialectValue
entries.Local behavior: Local config normalizes values into arrays/optional strings.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream preserves source JSON variants in typed enums.
entries.Impact: Upstream representation is clearer and more lossless.

entries.Title: Reusable generic StreamingWAVWriter buffer
entries.Local behavior: Local generic writer uses per-chunk buffers.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream reuses buffers and supports direct MLX/data writes.
entries.Impact: Useful only if generic writer remains supported.

entries.Title: Newer MLX/MLX-LM and Swift tools
entries.Local behavior: Local remains on the benchmarked exact pins.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has advanced to Swift tools 6.2 and newer MLX-LM.
entries.Impact: Security/compatibility fixes may accumulate, but blind upgrades can change quantization and numerics.

entries.Title: Newer MLXNN convolution wrapper refactor
entries.Local behavior: Local uses the older imported wrapper compatible with 0.30.6.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream subclasses current MLXNN convolution classes.
entries.Impact: Potentially simplifies future maintenance; tightly dependency-coupled.
entries.Disposition: Evaluate with dependency upgrade and numerical streaming tests.

entries.Title: Broad model/task support
entries.Local behavior: The fork is Qwen3-TTS-only.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream continues adding TTS, STT, VAD, STS, codec, and G2P models.
entries.Impact: Upstream is superior as a general SDK; irrelevant breadth would increase product risk.

entries.Title: third_party_patches understates ownership
entries.Local behavior: The directory name suggests a thin patch set even though the tree is intentionally specialized and locally owned.
entries.Impact: Can lead contributors to attempt unsafe rebases or mass replacement.
entries.Disposition: Rename conceptually now; physical move only with a dedicated migration.

entries.Title: UPSTREAM.md records import but not exhaustive patch ledger
entries.Local behavior: The metadata names the baseline and several retained deltas, but not every current symbol-level divergence.
entries.Impact: Forces archaeology when reviewing upstream changes.
entries.Disposition: Adopt the generated JSON/CSV ledger from this report.

entries.Title: Vendored README can describe removed upstream products/models
entries.Local behavior: The copied upstream-facing README is not a reliable description of the specialized package.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream README correctly describes the full SDK.
entries.Impact: Misleads contributors and package consumers.
entries.Disposition: Replace with a specialization README linking to provenance.

entries.Title: Stringly typed metrics and flags
entries.Local behavior: Many load/preparation/hot-loop values are merged by string keys across layers.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream has much less instrumentation and therefore less schema burden.
entries.Impact: Renames can silently break summarizers and historical comparisons.
entries.Disposition: Define typed metrics and one versioned serialization adapter.

entries.Title: Environment variables read inside vendor runtime
entries.Local behavior: Sampling, cache, and diagnostic policies are partly resolved from process environment in the modified package.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream uses generic call parameters and package defaults.
entries.Impact: Process-global policy is difficult to reason about and test under concurrency.
entries.Disposition: Resolve typed policy in BackendCore and inject it.

entries.Title: Qwen3TTS.swift combines many responsibilities
entries.Local behavior: One large source file contains caches, serialization, concurrency, APIs, prompt assembly, generation, sampling, diagnostics, and loading.
entries.Upstream behavior: Upstream file is also large but materially smaller and less product-specific.
entries.Impact: Raises review and selective-sync cost.
entries.Disposition: Split by responsibility under one model type.
